div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding-top: 17px; margin-left: 2px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 22px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-weight: bold; white-space: nowrap;

div.div: Batocera Swarm — enable_edge = FALSE (no Edge deployed)

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 1510px; height: 1px; padding-top: 55px; margin-left: 10px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 13px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: Drones use Overmind for coordination ONLY (outbound HTTPS). ROM/BIOS/save bytes move Drone↔Drone directly. The TransportSelector has just two tiers — [ LAN-direct → direct-public ]. The relay and hole-punch tiers need the Edge, so they are absent here. Result: full P2P on the SAME LAN; cross-network P2P only to a port-forwarded drone.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 728px; height: 1px; padding-top: 145px; margin-left: 402px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 13px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-weight: bold; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: CONTROL PLANE — Overmind (FastAPI · uvicorn/EC2 or AWS Lambda) — never carries ROM bytes

div.div:
Drone A — RECEIVER
TransportSelector.fetch:
1) LAN-direct
2) direct-public
(one active download · resume · hash verify)

Drone B — SOURCE
serves GET /peer/{roms,bios,saves}
mTLS · path-traversal-safe
(same LAN as A)

div.div:
Drone C — SOURCE (different network)
serves /peer/* (mTLS)
Reachable from the WAN ONLY if the
router PORT-FORWARDS :443 → C.

div.div:
LAN-direct ✓
mTLS GET → B.local_ip /peer/*
same public_ip ⇒ same LAN

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding-top: 590px; margin-left: 670px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000; background-color: #ffffff;

div.div:
direct-public — mTLS → C's public IP
✓ ONLY if C port-forwarded
✗ otherwise — NO relay fallback (transfer fails)

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 550px; height: 1px; padding-top: 843px; margin-left: 30px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
LEGEND
── blue = control-plane HTTPS (coordination; no ROM bytes)
── green (thick) = working P2P data path (mTLS /peer/*)
── red dashed = path that works ONLY with port-forwarding
yellow box = the auto-on reachability probe

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 590px; height: 1px; padding-top: 843px; margin-left: 670px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
WHEN enable_edge = FALSE FITS
• All drones on ONE LAN → full P2P, free, no Edge, no router config.
• Multi-site → only PORT-FORWARDED drones are reachable across the WAN
(the legacy model). Non-forwarded remote drones cannot sync.
For no-router-config cross-network sync, set enable_edge = true
(see edge-on-holepunch-relay-p2p.drawio).
